lens: Causal-inference / training-data-attribution (TDA) expert reviewing for a top ML/NLP venue (COLM/NeurIPS/ACL). I evaluated whether leave-cluster-out re-SFT yields a defensible causal estimand, whether the size-matched control identifies it, whether few-run statistics are valid, whether this is datamodels/influence rebranded, and whether the prior-work positioning (esp. the concurrent OLMo-2 attribution paper) survives scrutiny. I verified the load-bearing numbers and claims via web/HF/arXiv rather than trusting the draft.
major_concerns: FALSE DIFFERENTIATOR vs the closest scoop, repeated as a centerpiece. The proposal asserts (Sec 1 table, Sec 1 prose, Sec 8 threat table) that Probe-Based Data Attribution / 'In-the-Wild Model Organisms' (arXiv 2602.11079, now v2, OLMo 2 *7B* not unspecified) does NOT report a size-matched random-removal control. I verified the paper: it explicitly reports a random baseline (remove 30,000 random datapoints -> 6.67% harmful vs 7.63% baseline vs 2.86% for their method). The proposal's single biggest claimed novelty ('size-matched random-removal control their write-up does not report' -- elevated to 'the centerpiece' in Sec 3.3 and a column in the comparison table) is factually wrong. This is the kind of misstatement a reviewer who knows the scoop paper will catch immediately and it damages trust in the entire related-work section. The differentiation must be re-grounded on what is actually true (token-mass + per-source-skill matching may be more rigorous than their plain random draw, but that is a refinement, not a missing control).

SCOOP IS CLOSER THAN ADMITTED ON TWO MORE AXES. Verified from 2602.11079v2: (a) it ALSO does unsupervised discovery of MULTIPLE behaviors by clustering the behavior-datapoint similarity matrix (formatting, verbosity, harmful compliance, refusal-preference clusters), so the proposal's framing that the competitor studies 'a single safety regression' while 'we study a taxonomy' is half-true at best -- the competitor's *method* is multi-behavior, it just foregrounds one; (b) it reports a discrete dose-response (3k/12k/30k datapoints). So three of the four claimed differentiators (random control, taxonomy, dose-response) are weaker than stated. The genuinely surviving differentiators are: SFT-stage target (they ablate DPO), pro-social/safety-promoting behaviors (they study undesirable ones), external lexical/corpus auditable selector (they use opaque activation probes), and the pretraining-vs-SFT layer split (they do not have a pretraining corpus layer). These four are real and defensible -- the proposal should stake the novelty claim ONLY on these and drop the overclaims.

IDENTIFICATION: the size-matched random control does NOT fully identify the causal estimand the proposal wants. Matching on count + token mass + per-source skill distribution controls for data *volume* and *coarse skill mix*, but the targeted cluster differs from random-matched data on a confound that matching cannot remove: the targeted cluster is, by construction, the most *semantically coherent/low-diversity* slice (it is a tight HDBSCAN cluster), whereas a size-matched random draw is maximally diverse. Removing a coherent cluster vs. removing diffuse data are not exchangeable even at equal mass -- coherent-cluster removal changes the *effective number of distinct demonstrations of nearby behaviors* far more. The distractor-cluster arm (equally coherent, equal size, unrelated) is the control that actually addresses this, so the distractor arm -- not the random arm -- should be the headline DiD comparator. As written, the random arm is the centerpiece and the distractor arm is a 3-seed afterthought (C: 'Distractor-cluster-100 ... Seeds 3'). The identification argument is backwards: random removal answers 'is it data volume?', distractor removal answers 'is it *this* behavior cluster specifically?' -- the latter is the harder and more important confound, and it is under-powered.

REDUNDANCY / OVER-DETERMINATION will likely produce an attenuated or null primary effect, and the design has no mechanism to find the true causal footprint when it does. The proposal cites Simfluence's non-additivity correctly and even names this risk (Sec 8 'over-determination'), but the only mitigation offered ('at 100% dose consider removing the behavior across ALL enriched clusters') is hand-waved and not in the run matrix or budget. With an 866k-conversation mixture and a behavior as diffuse as empathy/disclaimers, a single densest cluster is very plausibly <20% of the behavior's data footprint, so even 100% targeted removal of one cluster may leave the behavior near-intact -- and the proposal's own 'publishable either way' framing then leans on a null being interesting. A null *can* be interesting, but only if you have ruled out 'we just didn't remove enough.' That requires an iterative/greedy multi-cluster ablation (remove all enriched clusters), which multiplies the GPU budget and is not costed. This is the single most likely reason the headline experiment underwhelms, and it is the weakest-defended part of the plan.

FEW-RUN STATISTICS: the variance partition is not as clean as claimed, and power is probably insufficient at the proposed seed counts. The plan treats SFT seed (5 for anchors, 3 for interior) as the cluster/randomization unit and proposes a cluster bootstrap over seeds + eval prompts. With only 5 seeds in the top arm and 3 in the interior arms, the seed-level bootstrap has ~5 effective units for the headline contrast -- a hierarchical bootstrap over 3-5 top-level clusters is known to be anti-conservative and unstable (the BCa interval can be undefined). The GLMM with (1|seed) estimates a variance component from 3-5 levels, which is essentially unidentified (mixed-model folklore: <~5-8 groups -> unreliable random-effect variance, biased fixed-effect SEs). The honest move is to (a) treat seed variance as the bottleneck and budget for >=8-10 seeds on the *two* headline arms even at the cost of dropping interior doses, or (b) explicitly adopt a fixed-effects-per-seed + permutation-test design and stop claiming GLMM random effects. The pilot is described as 4 runs (2+2) to 'estimate variance' -- 2 seeds per arm cannot estimate a variance with any precision; the pilot should be >=4 seeds/arm or it cannot do the job it is assigned (gating the whole budget).

CIRCULARITY is only partially defused. The lexicon screen is used both to (a) SELECT/label which cluster to ablate and (b) as one of three behavior instruments. Freezing the cluster IDs and eval set before training does NOT break this: if the cluster is selected for being enriched in phrase X, and the lexical instrument detects phrase X, then the lexical channel of the triangulated metric is mechanically guaranteed to move under ablation regardless of whether any *behavior* changed. The proposal demotes lexicon to '1 of 3' but still reports it. The clean fix is to EXCLUDE the lexical instrument from the primary outcome entirely (use it only for selection) and define the headline behavior delta on EPITOME/ESConv/JMIR + human-validated judge only -- otherwise reviewers will discount the effect as partly tautological. As written, the primary metric stack still contains the selection signal.

SELECTOR-VALIDITY EXPERIMENT (RQ5, tracer vs probe vs LESS) is the most novel and reviewer-disarming contribution, but it is dramatically under-resourced and the null it tests is a weak target. (a) It is not in the run matrix at all -- it would require >=3 additional leave-cluster-out re-SFT arms (probe-selected, LESS-selected, possibly x seeds) that are absent from the 33-run table and the budget; the GPU cost is undercounted. (b) The hypothesis H5 ('statistically indistinguishable deltas') is an equivalence claim, which with 3-5 seeds you are nowhere near powered to establish -- failing to reject is not equivalence. (c) Running LESS and an activation probe themselves requires gradient/internals infrastructure, partially undercutting the 'internals-free, single-CPU-workstation' selling point. Either properly cost and power this (it is worth it -- it directly defuses the scoop) or demote it to clearly-labeled future work, but do not present an underpowered equivalence test as a defusing experiment.
minor: Compute anchor VERIFIED and accurate: open-instruct docs/olmo2.md states 'SFT: 9 hours, 1 H100 node (8 GPUs)' = 72 H100-GPU-h/run exactly as claimed; hyperparameters (seq 4096, bs 2, grad-accum 8, LR 3e-5, 2 epochs) all match. Mixture size 866,138 conversations / 1.27 GB / 19 sources VERIFIED on HF. Base checkpoint allenai/OLMo-2-0425-1B VERIFIED to exist (with -SFT/-DPO/-Instruct variants). EPITOME (ER/IP/EX, Sharma EMNLP 2020) and ESConv (Liu ACL 2021) VERIFIED. Good rigor on these; keep the verified labels and citations.

The single-A100-80GB / ~60 GPU-h-per-run single-GPU throughput figure underpinning the headline ~$1.6-2k budget is NOT verified against this stack (the proposal honestly flags +/-50%). The 72 H100-h figure is multi-GPU wall-clock; converting to single-A100 hours is an extrapolation. The pilot-gating is the right instinct -- keep the headline budget as a RANGE and lead with the verified 8xH100 number ($4.9-5.8k for 27 runs) as the defensible figure, with single-GPU as the optimistic case.

Cite the scoop paper by its v2 title 'In-the-Wild Model Organisms: Mitigating Undesirable Emergent Behaviors in Production LLM Post-Training via Data Attribution' and correct model size to OLMo 2 7B (validated on 32B + OLMo 3 7B). The current draft's title and the implied small scale are stale.

JMIR 2026 5-level disclaimer scale, Nat. Mach. Intell. 2506.10150 judge-vs-expert alpha figures, Miller 'Adding Error Bars to Evals' 2411.00640, and the NIST Feb-2026 eval-stats report are cited as load-bearing but I did not verify them; flag these as to-be-confirmed and pin exact numbers/quartiles before submission (the proposal anchors its minimum-effect-of-interest to the '63% precedent' and '97% disclaimer base rate' -- both should be double-checked, the 63% is verified, the 97% is not).

Verbosity confound mitigation ('regress out length') interacts badly with the outcome: empathy/disclaimer behaviors are partly *expressed through* added length, so regressing out length may regress out the very effect. Prefer reporting both length-adjusted and unadjusted, and pre-specify which is primary, rather than treating length as a pure nuisance.

AlpacaEval 2 listed as a capability-regression placebo is itself an LLM-judged metric with high variance and length bias -- weak as a 'must not move' control. Prefer the deterministic benchmarks (MMLU-Pro, GSM8K, HumanEval+, IFEval) for the placebo claim.

Pythia/The-Pile generalization arm is correctly downgraded (no official matched SFT pipeline), but if kept it should be explicitly labeled 'constructed-SFT analogue, not a deployed-model ablation' in the abstract-level framing too, not only in the threat table, to avoid a reviewer reading it as a second deployed-model result.
what_would_change_mind: Correct the factual misstatement about the scoop paper's random control and re-stake novelty on the four claims that actually survive (SFT-stage vs DPO; pro-social/safety-promoting taxonomy vs undesirable behaviors; external lexical/corpus auditable selector vs activation probe; pretraining-vs-SFT layer split). If the differentiation is honest and the head-to-head selector experiment is properly run, the contribution stands -> +1 to +2.

Make the DISTRACTOR-cluster (coherent, equal-size, unrelated) the headline DiD comparator with full seed power, and relegate plain random removal to a secondary 'is it volume?' check. Demonstrating targeted-cluster removal beats *coherent-distractor* removal (not just random) is the result that actually rules out the 'coherent-slice removal is generically disruptive' confound -> would move soundness from 3 to 4.

Show, in the pilot, that seed-to-seed behavior-rate variance is small enough that the chosen seed counts give the pre-registered minimum effect (>=15-20pp) >80% power under a permutation test -- OR re-allocate the budget to >=8-10 seeds on the two headline arms. A credible power analysis grounded in measured (not assumed) seed variance is the difference between 'few-run causal inference' and 'underpowered anecdote.'

Add an iterative multi-cluster (remove-all-enriched-clusters) ablation arm, costed and budgeted, so that a null primary result can be attributed to genuine over-determination rather than 'removed too little.' This converts the 'publishable either way' claim from rhetorical to real.

Define the primary behavior outcome on the non-lexical instruments only (EPITOME/ESConv/JMIR + human-validated judge), with the lexicon used strictly for selection, eliminating the residual tautology between selector and metric.

Either fully power and cost the tracer-vs-probe-vs-LESS head-to-head (it is the strongest reviewer-disarming move) with an equivalence-test design that is actually powered, or demote it to future work -- but stop presenting an underpowered 'indistinguishable deltas' claim as a defusing experiment.
Report a measured single-GPU per-run wall-clock from the pilot so the headline budget is grounded rather than a +/-50% extrapolation; lead with the verified 8xH100 cost as the defensible number.

lens: Pragmatic top-venue area chair (COLM/NeurIPS/ICML/ACL), evaluating feasibility, novelty against concurrent work, and whether the causal claim is actually deliverable as scoped.
major_concerns: NOVELTY VS THE CONCURRENT SCOOP IS NARROWER THAN THE PROPOSAL CLAIMS, AND THE PROPOSAL'S OWN DESCRIPTION OF IT IS STALE. I verified arXiv 2602.11079 (Goodfire): it is now at v3 (revised 27 Apr 2026), not the 'Feb 2026' the proposal cites, and it uses OLMo 2 *7B*, not the size the proposal's comparison table leaves blank. It already does causal counterfactual retraining on OLMo 2 post-training data, validated by actual retraining. The proposal's four differentiators (SFT-not-DPO; pro-social taxonomy; lexical-not-probe selector; layer split + random control) are real but are *increments on an existing causal-retraining paradigm*, not a new paradigm. An AC will read the headline ('first end-to-end causal account', 'first reproducible cluster-level counterfactual') as overclaiming relative to a paper that is 4 months old by submission and will likely have a camera-ready by then. The contribution is a well-engineered replication-with-variations plus a cheaper auditable selector. That is publishable, but the framing must be de-escalated from 'first causal' to 'first SFT-stage, pro-social-behavior, auditable-selector causal study with a properly-controlled DiD' or reviewers will feel oversold.

STATISTICAL POWER WITH 3-5 SEEDS IS THE REAL RISK, AND THE PLAN PARTLY PAPERS OVER IT. The honest difficulty: the causal estimand (targeted-minus-random DiD) lives at the *training-run* level, and there are only 5 vs 5 runs at the 100% dose. The GLMM with (1|seed)+(1|eval_prompt) borrows power from eval prompts, but the between-run training variance is the term that actually gates the DiD CI, and 5 clusters per arm is a thin basis for a random effect — variance components are poorly estimated below ~5-8 groups and the GLMM can collapse the seed variance to ~0, producing anti-conservative CIs. The hierarchical bootstrap resampling 5 seeds is similarly unstable. If real per-run behavior-rate SD is moderate (say 5-10 points), the study may be underpowered to resolve a DiD smaller than its pre-registered 15-20pt MEI, and the whole paper rests on one contrast clearing significance. The pilot (4 runs) is the right instinct but 2 baseline + 2 targeted cannot estimate the *random-arm* variance that the DiD needs. Recommend: the pilot must include the random arm, and the budget should treat 'add seeds until the seed-variance estimate stabilizes' as a contingency line, not an afterthought.

THE SELECTOR-VALIDITY EXPERIMENT (RQ5) IS THE PAPER'S STRONGEST IDEA BUT IS BUDGETED AS OPTIONAL/UNDERSPECIFIED. The single most novel, scoop-defusing contribution is showing a cheap internals-free lexical/corpus selector picks a cluster whose ablation matches what an expensive probe (Goodfire) or LESS (gradients) picks. That is the result that turns 'replication with a different selector' into 'we made causal data attribution auditable and 10x cheaper.' Yet it requires running leave-cluster-out re-SFT on probe-selected AND LESS-selected clusters too — that is +10-20 more runs not in the 33-run matrix, and the budget table does not carry them. As written, RQ5 is asserted but unfunded. Either promote it to the spine (and add ~$1.5-3k of runs) or drop the strong claim that it 'defuses the strongest already-done objection.'

CLUSTER DEFINITION IS THE HIDDEN LOAD-BEARING STEP AND IS UNDER-DERISKED. The entire causal claim depends on HDBSCAN over 866k embedded assistant turns yielding a *coherent, enriched, removable* behavior cluster of tractable size. Risks the proposal underplays: (a) empathy/disclaimer language is diffuse and high-frequency, so the 'cluster' may be a large fraction of the mixture (removing it = a volume shock the random control cannot fully match on *content*, only on count/tokens/source); (b) HDBSCAN on 866k high-dim points is memory-heavy and notoriously parameter-sensitive — 'CPU-feasible overnight' is optimistic without dimensionality reduction and is unverified; (c) if the behavior is realized by single sentences embedded in otherwise-diverse conversations, *removing whole conversations* removes far more than the behavior, re-introducing the very confound the design exists to kill. A sentence-level vs conversation-level ablation-granularity decision is missing and is central.

BASE-MODEL FLOOR (RQ4) MAY UNDERCUT THE HEADLINE, AND THE PROPOSAL TREATS BOTH OUTCOMES AS WINS A BIT TOO COMFORTABLY. If the 1B base already emits empathy/disclaimers at a non-trivial rate (plausible: 4T-token pretraining contains enormous advice/forum text, and the proposal itself argues the phrasing is present in pretraining for RQ4), then the SFT-installed *increment* is small, and a targeted-minus-random DiD on a small increment is exactly the underpowered regime above. The 'over-determination is also interesting' escape hatch is true but weaker than the proposal implies: a null DiD that is indistinguishable from 'underpowered' is not a clean redundancy result. The paper needs the base-floor measurement *before* committing GPU money (it is correctly in M1) AND a decision rule: if base-floor is high, pivot to a behavior with a larger SFT increment (e.g. crisis-referral / structured ESConv strategies), chosen during M1, not post-hoc.
minor: FACTUAL FIXES I VERIFIED: the scoop (2602.11079) is OLMo 2 *7B* and is at *v3 (27 Apr 2026)*, not '1B'/'Feb 2026' — fix the comparison-table row and the §1/§8 text before any reviewer does. The 84% figure ('removing 4 worst generators') should be double-checked against v3; I confirmed 63% and 78% verbatim but the 84% was not visible in the abstract I fetched.

EVERYTHING ELSE I SPOT-CHECKED IS ACCURATE AND WELL-SOURCED: SFT mixture = 866,138 conv / 1.27 GB / 19 subsets (exact); open-instruct olmo2.md = '9 hours, 1 H100 node (8 GPUs)' = 72 GPU-h, LR 3e-5, 2 epochs, bs2 x ga8, seqlen 4096 (verbatim); LESS = ICML 2024 gradient-similarity select-to-add; EPITOME = 3 mechanisms (Sharma EMNLP 2020); ESConv = Liu ACL 2021; LoRA 'learns less forgets less' = arXiv 2405.09673; H100 SXM ~$2.50-2.69 on-demand / ~$1.03-1.19 spot, A100-80GB ~$1.07 on-demand / $0.60 spot; single-A100 1B full-FT feasible (~16GB/1B). The budget is honest and the +-50% caveat is appropriate. This is unusually well-grounded for a proposal.

Per-run wall-clock for *single-GPU subsampled* full FT (the ~60 GPU-h, ~$1.6-2k headline) is an extrapolation from the 8xH100 multi-node number; single-GPU avoids ZeRO-3 comms but loses 8-way data parallelism, so 60 GPU-h is plausible but unmeasured. Keep it explicitly pilot-gated as you do; do not lead the abstract with the lowest ($100-300 frugal-floor) figure.

The LLM-judge 'self-preference bias is structurally absent because OLMo is in no judge's family' argument is good but incomplete: judges still share *stylistic* priors (verbosity, hedging) that correlate with the very behaviors being scored — keep the human-IAA anchor as the primary defense, not the family argument.
AlpacaEval 2 listed under capability-regression is itself an LLM-judge metric and will move with verbosity/style; treat it as a style check, not a clean capability placebo.

Pythia/The-Pile arm is correctly demoted, but note there is no official matched SFT pipeline, so calling it 'replication' overstates it — it is a constructed-SFT analogue; the proposal mostly says this but the abstract/§1 still imply two-corpus symmetry.

Consider pre-registering the cluster *granularity* (sentence vs conversation) and the enrichment-ratio threshold that qualifies a cluster as 'the behavior cluster' — currently 'enriched vs base rate' is unquantified.
what_would_change_mind: A pilot (>=6 runs incl. the random arm: 2 baseline + 2 targeted-100 + 2 random-100) reporting the empirical between-run SD of the behavior rate, showing the pre-registered 15-20pt MEI is detectable with the budgeted seed count — this is the single most decisive evidence and converts borderline to accept.

An M1 cluster-feasibility result on the real 866k mixture: the actual size, coherence (silhouette/density), and enrichment ratio of the empathy/disclaimer/crisis clusters, plus the base-model floor for each candidate behavior — demonstrating at least one behavior with a large SFT-installed increment and a removable cluster that is a small, content-matched fraction of the mixture.

Promoting RQ5 (tracer vs probe vs LESS vs random, all via the same leave-cluster-out re-SFT) to a funded centerpiece with runs in the matrix and budget — this is what makes the paper a methods contribution rather than a replication, and directly neutralizes the scoop.

A de-escalated framing that positions the work explicitly relative to 2602.11079-v3 (cite as prior, not merely concurrent, given its April 2026 revision) and claims the *conjunction* (SFT stage + pro-social taxonomy + auditable selector + DiD with size/token/source-matched random control + pretraining-vs-SFT split) rather than 'first causal'.

A concrete decision on ablation granularity (conversation-level vs sentence/turn-level surgical removal) with a controlled comparison showing conversation-level removal does not itself constitute the volume/diversity confound.

lens: Novelty + experimental-design reviewer for data-attribution / alignment-behavior-origin work (NeurIPS/ICML/COLM), focused on whether the causal claim is genuinely new versus concurrent OLMo-2 counterfactual-retraining work, and whether the design actually licenses the causal conclusion at the proposed scale and budget.
major_concerns: NOVELTY IS NARROW AND DEFENSIVE, NOT GENERATIVE. I verified the scoop (arXiv 2602.11079v2): it does counterfactual retraining on OLMo 2, with probe + LESS selectors, reporting 63%/78%/85% reductions. The proposal's four differentiators are all real (it ablates DPO not SFT; one safety regression not a pro-social taxonomy; probe not lexical selector; no size-matched random control; no layer split) but they are a *delta* on a published method, not a new idea. The intellectual core reduces to 'leave-cluster-out retraining is the gold standard,' which the proposal itself concedes the entire datamodels/TRAK literature already treats as ground truth. A novelty-focused reviewer will read this as: correct, careful, useful confirmation/extension, but the central mechanism (retrain-without-cluster -> behavior delta) is not novel. The novelty lives almost entirely in (a) the lexical/auditable selector and (b) the pretraining-vs-SFT dissociation. The paper must be reframed so THOSE are the headline, not 'first causal account.'

THE 'CAUSAL' UPGRADE OVER THE EXISTING TOOL IS SMALLER THAN ADVERTISED, BECAUSE THE SELECTOR IS STILL CORRELATIONAL. The tracer selects clusters by lexical enrichment, then re-SFT tests them. But the causal estimand is about *the cluster*, not *the tracer*. If the tracer picks a cluster and ablation works, that validates 'this cluster causes the behavior,' not 'lexical provenance is a good causal selector' — unless RQ5 (head-to-head vs probe/LESS) is run, and RQ5 is listed as optional/Tier-2. Without RQ5 in the core, the contribution over the scoop collapses to 'we ablated different clusters in a different stage,' which is incremental. RQ5 must be promoted to a primary, non-optional experiment; it is the single result that makes the lexical-selector contribution stand on its own.

OVER-DETERMINATION / REDUNDANCY IS THE MOST LIKELY OUTCOME AND THE DESIGN UNDER-PREPARES FOR IT. The proposal's own framing (Simfluence non-additivity; behavior latent in a 4T-token base; 'empathy examples cover for each other') predicts that removing ONE densest cluster will produce a small or null delta because the behavior is distributed across many SFT subsets AND pretraining. The proposal acknowledges this and says 'a null is also publishable as a redundancy result' — but a borderline-to-reject reviewer will note that a null DiD at 1B is hard to distinguish from (i) insufficient ablation coverage, (ii) seed noise swamping a small effect, and (iii) the behavior being a generic instruction-following artifact. The 'remove across ALL enriched clusters' fallback is mentioned only in the threats table; it should be a pre-registered primary arm, because the single-cluster experiment has a real chance of being uninterpretable.

STATISTICAL POWER AT 3-5 SEEDS IS THE QUIET KILLER AND IS ASSERTED, NOT DEMONSTRATED. The whole causal claim is a difference-in-differences whose error is dominated by seed-to-seed SFT variance (the cluster/random arms differ by ~0.01-0.05% of the data at interior doses). The GLMM with (1|seed) borrows strength across eval prompts, but the seed random effect is estimated from 3-5 points per cell — the variance component itself is barely identified, and the headline contrast's CI hinges on it. The pilot (4 runs) estimates a wall-clock number but CANNOT estimate seed variance reliably (n=2 baseline). There is no power analysis tying expected ΔΔ (anchored at tens of pp) to seed SD to the n=5 design. If seed SD on the behavior rate is even ~5-8 pp, the n=5 DiD CI will straddle zero for anything but a huge effect. A real power simulation (assume plausible seed SD, simulate the GLMM, report MDE) is mandatory and currently absent.

THE PRETRAINING-VS-SFT 'LAYER DISSOCIATION' (RQ4) — the most genuinely novel piece — IS UNDER-SPECIFIED AND POSSIBLY UNFALSIFIABLE AS STATED. 'Phrasing present in pretraining but behavior installed at SFT' conflates two non-equivalent measurements: verbatim n-gram counts in Dolma (phrasing-level) vs. behavior-emission rate (behavior-level). A high infini-gram count for 'consult a professional' is almost guaranteed for any common English phrase, so H4's 'present in pretraining' clause is nearly always trivially true and carries little information. The interesting quantity is whether the base model EMITS the behavior in context, which is a generation measurement, not a count. As written, RQ4 risks being a foregone conclusion (counts high, base-rate low) that doesn't actually dissociate a mechanism. It needs a sharper operationalization: e.g., does few-shot prompting the BASE model recover the behavior (latent capability) vs. does only SFT install it (acquired) — and a quantitative pretraining-influence comparison, not just a count.
minor: The proposal says the scoop's write-up 'does not report' a size-matched random control, but 2602.11079 Table 2 DOES have a 'Random' baseline (random ranking, not size-matched). The claim is technically defensible but currently overstated in the prose ('do not report a size-matched random-removal control' is fine; 'size-matched random control their write-up does not report' is fine; but elsewhere it reads as 'no random control,' which is false). Tighten to avoid a reviewer catching an inaccuracy.

The single-GPU full-FT memory line ('~16-20 GB optimizer states; rule-of-thumb ~16 GB/1B') understates the true footprint: AdamW full FT of 1B in bf16 is roughly model(2GB)+grads(2GB)+optimizer-fp32-states(~12GB)+master-weights, ~16-20GB for states alone but ~30-48GB total with activations. It fits on an A100-80GB comfortably (verified), but the budget prose should state TOTAL footprint, not just optimizer states, or a reviewer will distrust the rest of the budget.

The ±50% per-run uncertainty + pilot-gated budget is honest and good, but the headline '~$1.6-2k' assumes single-GPU full FT at ~60 GPU-h/run while the verified anchor is 72 H100-GPU-h on 8xH100 (i.e., ~9 wall-clock h with multi-GPU parallelism). Single-GPU wall-clock for 72 GPU-h of work is ~72h/run, i.e., 27 runs ~= 1944 GPU-h either way; the $ is right but the framing conflates GPU-hours with the convenience of multi-GPU wall-clock. State that single-GPU saves $ (no idle GPUs in a node) but costs calendar time (~72h/run), which matters for the 5-month timeline with 27-33 sequential runs on one GPU.

EPITOME, ESConv, and the 'JMIR 2026 5-level disclaimer scale' are cited as off-the-shelf instruments; EPITOME and ESConv are real and well-known, but verify the JMIR 2026 ordinal scale citation exists and is clinician-validated as claimed before submission — it is load-bearing for the safety-measurement triangulation and I could not confirm it.

Pythia/The-Pile arm is correctly demoted, but stating there is 'no official matched SFT pipeline' for Pythia is the right call — consider dropping the Pythia generalization arm entirely from the main proposal; it adds reviewer surface area ('constructed-SFT analogue') for little causal payoff and dilutes the 'only fully-open organism' framing.

AlpacaEval 2 / IFEval as capability-regression placebos partly overlap with the target behaviors (instruction-following and helpfulness correlate with empathy/structure); pick capability controls that are mechanistically orthogonal (GSM8K, HumanEval+, MMLU-Pro) and drop the chat-quality benchmarks from the placebo set, or a reviewer will argue the placebo can move for the same reason the target does.
what_would_change_mind: Promote RQ5 (lexical tracer vs probe vs LESS vs random, all under identical leave-cluster-out re-SFT) from optional to a PRIMARY experiment with allocated runs. This is the one result that converts 'we ran a careful counterfactual' into 'cheap auditable lexical provenance is a *valid causal selector*,' which is a genuinely new and defensible contribution distinct from the scoop. Without it, the paper is an extension; with it, the paper is a method-validation.

Add a real power/MDE simulation: assume a plausible seed SD for the behavior rate (e.g., bootstrap it from the 4-run pilot or from any published OLMo-2 SFT seed-variance number), simulate the GLMM DiD at n=3 and n=5, and report the minimum detectable ΔΔ. If MDE < expected effect with margin, the causal claim is powered; right now it is faith-based.

Pre-register the 'remove across ALL enriched clusters' arm as a primary condition (not a threats-table fallback), so the headline causal test targets the full behavioral footprint and a near-null single-cluster result becomes interpretable rather than ambiguous. Pair it with a base-model few-shot floor for RQ4 so over-determination vs. SFT-installation is cleanly separated.

Reframe the abstract and contributions so the headline is (a) the auditable lexical causal selector validated head-to-head and (b) the pretraining-vs-SFT dissociation — NOT 'first causal account,' which the concurrent OLMo-2 retraining paper undercuts.
